TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua ɫa maḍusa marekaka.
S.PHON: izua ʎa maɖusa marəkaka.
S.FORM: izua lja madrusa marekaka.
S.PHON: izua ʎa maɖusa marəkaka.

S.FORM: qau vaik a tjavulung a sema tjatjan.
S.PHON: qau vaik a cavuɭuŋ a səma cacan.
S.FORM: qau vaik a tjavulung a sema tjatjan.
S.PHON: qau vaik a ʦavuɭuŋ a səma ʦaʦan.
S.TRANSL: The older one went to the spring,

S.FORM: qau adjuqen i umaq a tjaɫaɫak a uqaɫay.
S.PHON: qau aɉuqən i umaq a caʎaʎak a uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: qau adjuqen i umaq a tjaljaljak a uqaljay.
S.PHON: qau aɉuqən i umaq a ʦaʎaʎak a uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: and left the younger brother at home.

S.FORM: qau mangtjez a tjavulung kasi tjatjan.
S.PHON: qau maŋcəz a cavuɭuŋ kasi cacan.
S.FORM: qau mangtjez a tjavulung kasi tjatjan.
S.PHON: qau maŋʦəz a ʦavuɭuŋ kasi ʦaʦan.
S.TRANSL: When the older one came back from the spring,

S.FORM: neka kaka; inalap na qaɫa, maulaw.
S.PHON: nəka kaka; inaɭap na qaʎa, mauɭaw.
S.FORM: neka kaka; inalap na qalja, maulaw.
S.PHON: nəka kaka; inaɭap na qaʎa, mauɭaw.
S.TRANSL: the brother wasn’t there; he had been taken by enemies and was gone.

S.FORM: qau qemaung a tjavulung: "paramur a semainu a kaka?"
S.PHON: qau qəmauŋ a cavuɭuŋ: "paramur a səmainu a kaka?"
S.FORM: qau qemaung a tjavulung: "paramur a semainu a kaka?"
S.PHON: qau qəmauŋ a ʦavuɭuŋ: "paramur a səmainu a kaka?"
S.TRANSL: The older one cried: “This is awful(?). Where has brother gone?”

S.FORM: vaik a kemim sa qauqaung.
S.PHON: vaik a kəmim sa qauqauŋ.
S.FORM: vaik a kemim sa qauqaung.
S.PHON: vaik a kəmim sa qauqauŋ.
S.TRANSL: She cried and went to look for him.

S.FORM: qau tevuta a tjatjiw kidjekets ta kasiw.
S.PHON: qau təvuta a caciw kiɉəkəʦ ta kasiw.
S.FORM: qau tevuta a tjatjiw kidjekec ta kasiw.
S.PHON: qau təvuta a ʦaʦiw kiɉəkəʦ ta kasiw.
S.TRANSL: Then a black drongo bird appeared and alighted on a tree.

S.FORM: qau "tjatjiw tjatjiw, pagaɫugaɫu a qemaung.
S.PHON: qau "caciw caciw, pagaʎugaʎu a qəmauŋ.
S.FORM: qau "tjatjiw tjatjiw, pagaljugalju a qemaung.
S.PHON: qau "ʦaʦiw ʦaʦiw, pagaʎugaʎu a qəmauŋ.

S.FORM: pazeliuɫi aken ta kaɫauɫavan.
S.PHON: pazəɭiuʎi akən ta kaʎauʎavan.
S.FORM: pazeliulji aken ta kaljauljavan.
S.PHON: pazəɭiuʎi akən ta kaʎauʎavan.

S.FORM: ku sikiliɫkauɫaw a su paɫekakain i tjuruzaquzaquin."
S.PHON: ku sikiɭiʎkauʎaw a su paʎəkakain i curuzaquzaquin."
S.FORM: ku sikililjkauljaw a su paljekakain i tjuruzaquzaquin."
S.PHON: ku sikiɭiʎkauʎaw a su paʎəkakain i ʦuruzaquzaquin."
S.TRANSL: and I’ll bring back (?) your brother from the sapindus wood.”

S.FORM: qau pavayin ta qalev.
S.PHON: qau pavajin ta qaɭəv.
S.FORM: qau pavayin ta qalev.
S.PHON: qau pavajin ta qaɭəv.
S.TRANSL: She gave the bird some oil.

S.FORM: qau ka maka qalev, qau senay: "tjatjiw tjatjiw, imazamaza-u.
S.PHON: qau ka maka qaɭəv, qau sənaj: "caciw caciw, imazamaza-u.
S.FORM: qau ka maka qalev, qau senay: "tjatjiw tjatjiw, imazamaza-u.
S.PHON: qau ka maka qaɭəv, qau sənaj: "ʦaʦiw ʦaʦiw, imazamaza-u.
S.TRANSL: When it had finished oiling itself, it sang: “Tjatjiw tjatjiw, stay here.

S.FORM: ku sikiliɫkauɫaw a su paɫekakain i tjuruzaquzaquin."
S.PHON: ku sikiɭiʎkauʎaw a su paʎəkakain i curuzaquzaquin."
S.FORM: ku sikililjkauljaw a su paljekakain i tjuruzaquzaquin."
S.PHON: ku sikiɭiʎkauʎaw a su paʎəkakain i ʦuruzaquzaquin."
S.TRANSL: I’ll bring back your brother from the sapindus wood.”

S.FORM: qau vaik a malap ta kaka aza tjatjiw.
S.PHON: qau vaik a maɭap ta kaka aza caciw.
S.FORM: qau vaik a malap ta kaka aza tjatjiw.
S.PHON: qau vaik a maɭap ta kaka aza ʦaʦiw.
S.TRANSL: So the bird went to get the brother.

S.FORM: tevuta a kaka kinatsukatsu na tjatjiw.
S.PHON: təvuta a kaka kinaʦukaʦu na caciw.
S.FORM: tevuta a kaka kinacukacu na tjatjiw.
S.PHON: təvuta a kaka kinaʦukaʦu na ʦaʦiw.
S.TRANSL: The brother appeared, carried by the bird.

S.FORM: qau avan a nu sikeɫang ta tjatjiw tu mamazangiɫan.
S.PHON: qau avan a nu sikəʎaŋ ta caciw tu mamazaŋiʎan.
S.FORM: qau avan a nu sikeljang ta tjatjiw tu mamazangiljan.
S.PHON: qau avan a nu sikəʎaŋ ta ʦaʦiw tu mamazaŋiʎan.
S.TRANSL: And that’s how we know that the black drongo bird is of chiefly rank.

S.FORM: qau inika qetsiqetsiin na Payuan a tjatjiw.
S.PHON: qau inika qəʦiqəʦiin na pajuan a caciw.
S.FORM: qau inika qeciqeciin na Payuan a tjatjiw.
S.PHON: qau inika qəʦiqəʦiin na pajuan a ʦaʦiw.
S.TRANSL: And Paiwan people don’t kill a black drongo bird.
